heading: Government seals wrongfully used and instruments wrongfully sealed
label: 1017 Government seals wrongfully used and instruments wrongfully sealed
text: Whoever fraudulently or wrongfully affixes or impresses the seal of any department or agency of the United States, to or upon any certificate, instrument, commission, document, or paper or with knowledge of its fraudulent character, with wrongful or fraudulent intent, uses, buys, procures, sells, or transfers to another any such certificate, instrument, commission, document, or paper, to which or upon which said seal has been so fraudulently affixed or impressed, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than five years, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 130 (, title X, § 1, ).June 15, 1917, ch. 3040 Stat. 227To clarify scope of section and in view of definition of department or agency in , words “department or agency” were substituted for “executive department, or of any bureau, commission, or office”.section 6 of this titleSlight verbal changes were also made.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $5,000”.Pub. L. 103–322
